_comment: Battle AI phase 3 (#586): the general's reserve pool and its reserve-commit morale threshold. Freshly authored for this phase -- not copied from any other PR's scripted-input file (see .claude/memories/sparta-demos.md's 'author each demo scenario fresh' entry). Forces team 1 onto the 'cautious' doctrine (data/doctrines/cautious.json: reserve_fraction 0.4, reserve_commit_morale_threshold 30.0, pursue_routers false) via the input script's own 'doctrine' field. Team 1 has 5 units, staged in uid order so three understrength, low-morale Infantry (uid 1-3, 30 soldiers, morale 32 -- just above the commit threshold) spawn before two full-strength units (uid 4-5, a Cavalry and an Infantry) -- General._reserve_uids holds back the highest-uid fraction (2 of 5), so uid 4-5 are the ones held in reserve, HOLD_LINE-pinned at their own spawn point (General.reserve_directives) far from the front (beyond UnitLeader.RELIEF_CALL_RANGE, so the ordinary inter-unit relief call -- ALSO a real mechanic, but a different one -- can't reach them and confound the demo; see this file's own PR discussion). Verified by state dump (tools/demo/dump-state.sh) at every 60-tick AI decision point: uid 1-3 alone meet the single full-strength enemy Infantry and take losses (uid 3 has routed by tick 240); uid 4-5 are STILL current_order null/IDLE at tick 240 (Battle._physics_process runs _run_enemy_ai before the Units' own _physics_process, so a tick's AI decision reads morale from before that same tick's combat -- the dump's tick-240 snapshot already reflects that tick's own damage, one step ahead of what the tick-240 decision itself saw). By the next AI decision tick (300), uid 4-5's current_order has flipped to ATTACK/MOVING -- the visible commit. The two reserves close at different rates: uid 4 (Cavalry) is FIGHTING/engaged by tick 540, but uid 5 (Infantry) is still MOVING/unengaged at tick 780 and only FIGHTING by tick 840 (per CI's own posted per-tick transcript for this PR -- a long chaotic battle like this one can diverge in its exact tick-by-tick numbers between a local headless run and CI's, so the CI-posted transcript, not a local dump-state.sh run, is the number to verify a precise-tick claim against). Static wide camera (no pan) keeps the whole field -- both the front clash and the waiting reserve pair -- in view throughout, so the hold-then-commit transition reads clearly without needing a mid-clip move.
